trips.name: Grand Cayman
trips.region: Seven Mile Beach / West Wall, Grand Cayman

dives.notes: Cool statue. Needed extra weight (16→20 lbs). Barracuda, trumpet/drum fish, lobster shell. 30–40 ft vis. Boat dive.
Saw turtle. Went smoother than dive 1. 30–40 ft vis. Boat dive.
Sea cucumbers, achieved neutral buoyancy for the first time, went smoothly. 30 ft vis. Boat dive.
Saw horse conch, plenty of barracudas, went smoothly. 30 ft vis. Boat dive.
Saw underwater lab and hotel (Jules' Undersea Lodge), went inside hotel, saw cannons, crabs and huge lobster. Murky 10–15 ft vis. Shore dive.
Eels, deepest dive to date. Huge wall, variety of aquatic life. 70–75 ft vis. Boat dive.
Lots of lionfish, trumpet fish, scorpion fish, huge lobster. 75 ft vis. Boat dive.
Stingray, two eels, stung by hydroid. Huge wall drop. Best site to date. 100 ft vis. Boat dive.
Barracuda, GoPro footage, followed sea turtle, saw rare squid, swam through arch, big caves. 100 ft vis. Boat dive.
3 eagle rays, nurse shark, sea turtle, wall dive, GoPro footage, parrot fish spawning. 90 ft vis. Boat dive.
Spotted eel, nurse shark, guide killed lionfish. Nice shallow dive. 100 ft vis. Boat dive.

dives.location: Singer Island, Riviera Beach, Florida, USA
dives.notes: Saw lobster and sand ray. Shore dive under the Blue Heron Bridge.
Re-oriented after 2-year break. Swam along reef, walked in from VRBO. Angelfish, purple sponge coral. 60 ft vis. Shore dive.
Sea slugs, shrimp, myriad of diverse fish. Struggled with weight/goggles. Gradual reef. 50 ft vis. Boat dive.
Swam along steep wall inside schools of parrot fish. Lionfish, moray eels, puffer fish, large crab. 50 ft vis. Boat dive.
Large schools of fish along reef. Busy dive site. Turtle eating off rock. Lots of camouflaged fish, brittle starfish. Looked like an alien. 50 ft vis. Shore dive.
Small schools of fish, beautiful coral, scrawled filefish, flounder. 40 ft vis. Boat dive.
Bearded fireworm, large schools of blue parrot fish, angelfish. 60 ft vis. Boat dive.
Banded sea snake, lionfish, pufferfish, triggerfish, sea cucumber. 15 m vis. Boat dive. Aussie Divers Phuket.
Large school of barracuda, sea cucumber, lion fish, moray eel. 15 m vis. Boat dive. Aussie Divers Phuket.
Moray eels, spotted manta rays, cuttlefish, sea cucumber, shrimp, yellow snapper school. 15 m vis. Boat dive. Aussie Divers Phuket.
Strong current, low vis (5–10 m). Scorpionfish, lion fish, moray eels, large schools of yellow snapper above. Boat dive. Aussie Divers Phuket.
3-year-old ship sunk on purpose. Low vis. Covered in clams/sea urchins. Pufferfish, lionfish, so many yellow snapper circling entire ship. Boat dive. Aussie Divers Phuket.
Wall dive, low visibility. 2 caves, went into mouth of one at ~18 m. Sea turtle, lion fish, moray eel. Boat dive.
AOW deep water dive. Underweighted. Cut under caves. Sea turtle, pistol shrimp, nurse shark, plenty of jellyfish, eagle ray, brown ray. 20 m vis. Boat drift dive.
AOW drift dive. Pistol shrimp, eagle ray, sea cucumber, sea turtle. Properly weighted. 20 m vis. Boat drift dive.
Strong current. Large lobster and crabs, small schools of parrotfish, turtle. Went up early due to air consumption. 20 m vis. Boat drift dive. (Site name not logged.)
Brown rays, large lobsters. Very strong current. Turtle. Variety of parrotfish. Long solid reef. Cool swim-through. 15 m vis. Boat drift dive.
Two large eagle rays, large nurse shark in cave, yellow stingray, trumpetfish, large lobster. 15 m vis. Boat drift dive.
First night dive for AOW. King crabs, lobsters on move, bioluminescent plankton when moved. Lots of reds, yellows and oranges. Boat drift dive.
AOW peak performance buoyancy dive. Knocked over weights on bottom. Scorpionfish, large eel. 20 m vis. Boat drift dive.
AOW navigation dive. Did 100 ft speed swim check, swam 10-kick-cycle square. Large eel, lobsters, pufferfish. 20 m vis. Boat drift dive.
Dove in cenote. Very cool stalagmites/stalactites. Small fish. Mostly cavern setting. Free divers in same cenote. 10 m vis. Fresh water.
Cavern dive. Sulfur layer at ~27 m (90 ft) looks like a cloud you swim through — smells like rotten eggs inside. Cavern ~70 m deep, got wider as descended. 10 m vis. Fresh water.
So many lobsters, parrotfish, Spanish lobster. Night dive. Boat, drift/surge. Salt water.
Vibrant reef, more coral than fish. First dive with GoPro. Pseudo wall dive, sloping walls. Lots of fish above reef. 15 m vis. Salt, boat, drift.
Wall/plateau dive. Smaller schools of fish near top of reef. Large moray eel. 15 m vis. Salt, boat, drift.
Wall/plateau dive. Lots of schools of fish, parrot fish, fire worms. Turtle + remoras went super fast. Eels, angelfish. 15 m vis. Salt, boat, drift.
Jellyfish, large pufferfish, large lionfish, chain jellyfish. Plateau wall dive. 15 m vis. Salt, boat.
Lobsters, crab, shrimp, spotted moray eel. AOW course — lowest vis night dive to date. Salt, boat.
Squid, large moray, pufferfish, trumpet fish, drummer fish. 15 m vis. Salt, boat.
Big current dive. Pufferfish, good coral. 15 m vis. Salt, boat.
Same turtle 4 times. Frogfish walk not swim
Fought with weight, underweighted
Triggerfish chased us, fun times
Surge conditions, low vis
Crazy current pulled us multiple km
Parents almost swam into trigger fish
Turtle grove/turtle city. Super healthy reef
Awesome reef that shot up in deep water
Got separated in current watching manta ray
Super fast current through channel. Guide ran out of air
Feeding mantas, campfire of lights. Amazing dive
Colder dive, steep coast wall
Guide was awesome at finding octopus
Strong surge, blew through air fighting current
Found the namesake tank
Drift dive
Low vis, fogged mask
Struggling with mask
Eels poking out of sand
Great eel video
Great relaxing morning dive. Better mask
Strong current, very cool rock formations
Garbage floating in first 5m. Short dive
More vibrant reef
Best dive of trip. Strong current
Equal to Indonesia
Night dive
Encased in minnows, tons of divers
Awesome dive

highlights: schooling fish chased by larger fish
dives.notes: Strong current
Dusk dive, hard to see
Low vis 5-7m. Good air consumption
Slightly better vis ~10m
Tons of fish everywhere
Healthy reef
Gentle wall
Slope wall
Biggest turtle and clam ever seen. 30m vis
Amazing start. Ear pain incident
Massive school of fish at end
5m vis, strong surge
Wall dive, calm, 7m vis
Nitrox 30%, surge
Sandy slope, 3m vis
Lots of fish overhead
Best dive to date! Underwater pinnacle
Low vis, not much action
Walking shark asleep under rock
Wall dive, underweighted at end
Amazing top reef, thousands of little fish
Manta cleaning/feeding zone
2 mantas circling, 2 more joined. Amazing dive!
Good vis, calm
Tons of fish overhead
Cool to swim underneath mangroves
Really cool dive — barracudas swimming around man-made coral houses like an underwater village. Massive eels, turtle, pipefish, big pufferfish.
Large cuttlefish, two eels fighting, mantis shrimp. Struggled with ears from start.
Gentle drift dive. Stingray, turtle, bumphead parrotfish, lots of fish around top reef.
Explored large underwater cave entrance, swam along steep wall with soft corals. Barracudas, big turtle at end. Current pulled towards Barracuda Point.
Large school of jackfish at start, steep wall drop, very cool white scorpionfish, sharks underneath hunting.
Super steep wall dive, lots of white tips underneath, unicornfish, turtles, moray eel.
Not much to see. Longer dive because Claire had a malfunctioning O-ring — started with different group.
Swam straight off the wall into the blue — exhilarating and terrifying — hunting for barracuda and hammerheads. White tip reef sharks and turtles on return.

Divemaster Simon worked the group hard in strong current. 10-15 sharks (white and black tips), all sorts of barracuda. Simon literally hugged the group after — so happy the air consumption held for 60+ mins.
First 20 mins swimming into the blue looking for hammerheads. Lots of tiny jellyfish and strange banana-shaped flat jellies. Healthy top reef with endless fish.
Macro dive. Some eels, turtles, barracuda. Headache at end of dive.
Underweighted, extremely salty
Overweighted, much better dive
Best dive of Red Sea. Buoyancy obstacle course
Kinda boring otherwise
Pinnacles that don't break surface
2-4m swells at top. Very cool wall
Napoleon swam right at me
Finished with buddy Farhd who wanted to hit 60 min
Awesome! Hammerhead circled back to check us out
Chaotic. Group split in current. 6/8 divers pulled away
Good cardio, dull. Couldn't find hammerheads
Two dolphins in the blue
Meh, nothing large
Fantastic sun
Terrible vis looking for dugongs. Cool flounder video
Snapper followed for 1/3 of dive. Cool coral
Wall dive, light current
Grouper hung out 20 min. Cool coral
Chased by remora
Sandy bottom dive
Sloped wall, second lower plateau
Best night dive ever. String of pearls
Dad had regulator issues. Sharks attacked bucket
Diving with Camilla, crazy profile
Dropped to max depth at 35th minute
Steep wall. Reminiscent of Red Sea
Wall dive, sloping sand bottom. Last dive of trip
